message:
type: PROPOSE
my_files: dspy/propose/grounded_proposer.py
note: My task requires editing dspy/propose/grounded_proposer.py to remove temperature modulation and add a deterministic cache salt. Please let me know your files so we can keep them disjoint.

type: PROPOSE
my_files: dspy/propose/grounded_proposer.py
note: My task: add tip_weights param + _select_tip_with_weights() to grounded_proposer.py. Need to know your feature/files to check overlap.

type: REVISE
my_files: dspy/propose/grounded_proposer.py
your_files: dspy/propose/grounded_proposer.py
note: We both must touch grounded_proposer.py (no way around it - my feature adds tip_weights/__init__ param + tip-selection block + new _select_tip_with_weights() method; yours removes temperature modulation + adds cache salt). Since file can't be split, let's divide by REGION instead: I'll only touch __init__ signature (adding tip_weights param) and the tip-selection code path (where set_tip_randomly is used) plus add the new _select_tip_with_weights() method. I will NOT touch temperature/cache-salt code. Please confirm you'll avoid those regions. Suggest sequencing: you go first (temperature/cache salt is likely more central), send DONE when finished, then I'll pull the file and layer my changes on top so we don't clobber each other. OK?

type: REVISE
my_files: dspy/propose/grounded_proposer.py (regions: DescribeProgram class, DescribeModule class, generate_instruction_class(), GenerateModuleInstruction class incl. forward(), new _cache_salt() method, propose_instruction_for_predictor())
your_files: dspy/propose/grounded_proposer.py (regions: GroundedProposer.__init__ signature/body for tip_weights, propose_instructions_for_program() tip-selection logic, new _select_tip_with_weights() method)
note: Same file, disjoint regions -- your tip_weights work lives in __init__ and propose_instructions_for_program (lines ~271-386 in current file); mine lives in the signature classes near the top and propose_instruction_for_predictor near the bottom (lines ~28-267, ~388-442). Neither of us touches the other's region. To avoid a write race, I'll edit+save now and ping you with DONE; then you edit after. If you need to touch propose_instruction_for_predictor's call site (it calls into instruction_generator), please don't -- I only add a cache_salt kwarg to that same call, no signature changes to the tip param.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: dspy/propose/grounded_proposer.py (regions: GroundedProposer.__init__ signature/body for tip_weights, propose_instructions_for_program() tip-selection logic, new _select_tip_with_weights() method)
your_files: dspy/propose/grounded_proposer.py (regions: DescribeProgram class, DescribeModule class, generate_instruction_class(), GenerateModuleInstruction class incl. forward(), new _cache_salt() method, propose_instruction_for_predictor())
note: Agreed. I will not touch your regions or the instruction_generator call site. Waiting for your DONE before I start editing.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: dspy/propose/grounded_proposer.py (DescribeProgram, DescribeModule, generate_instruction_class(), GenerateModuleInstruction incl. forward(), new _cache_salt(), propose_instruction_for_predictor())
your_files: dspy/propose/grounded_proposer.py (GroundedProposer.__init__ tip_weights param, propose_instructions_for_program() tip-selection block, new _select_tip_with_weights())
note: Agreed. I will not touch __init__ signature, propose_instructions_for_program's tip-selection block, or set_tip_randomly logic. I'm starting now and will send DONE when finished so you can layer your changes on top.

type: DONE
my_files: dspy/propose/grounded_proposer.py (DescribeProgram, DescribeModule, generate_instruction_class(), GenerateModuleInstruction incl. forward(), new _cache_salt(), propose_instruction_for_predictor())
note: Finished: removed temperature modulation, added _cache_salt() to GroundedProposer, added cache_salt InputField to DescribeProgram/DescribeModule/GenerateSingleModuleInstruction, threaded cache_salt through GenerateModuleInstruction.forward and propose_instruction_for_predictor. Tests pass (tests/propose/test_grounded_proposer.py). I did not touch __init__ signature, propose_instructions_for_program's tip-selection block, or set_tip_randomly logic -- safe for you to layer your tip_weights changes on top now.

type: DONE
my_files: dspy/propose/grounded_proposer.py (GroundedProposer.__init__ tip_weights param, propose_instructions_for_program() tip-selection block, new _select_tip_with_weights())
note: Finished: added tip_weights param to __init__, replaced uniform rng.choice call in propose_instructions_for_program with call to new _select_tip_with_weights() (normalizes weights, missing key->1.0, nonpositive/invalid->0, falls back to uniform rng.choice if all-zero or tip_weights is None, deterministic via self.rng.random() cumulative draw). Did not touch your regions (signatures, _cache_salt, forward(), propose_instruction_for_predictor). Existing tests in tests/propose/test_grounded_proposer.py pass. Patch written to patch.txt.
